zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Thomas le Latimer於1270年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾其皮三 瓦登時，他的父親Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby26歲，母親Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook20歲。他於大約1292年與Lora de Hastings在英國諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1334年在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可享年64歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Thomas le Latimer于1270年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾其皮三 瓦登时，他的父亲Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby26岁，母亲Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook20岁。他于大约1292年与Lora de Hastings在英国諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1334年在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可享年64岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾布雷布盧可。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Latimer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a Latinist, a clerk who wrote documents in Latin, from Anglo-Norman French latinier, latim(m)ier. Latin was more or less the universal language of official documents in the Middle Ages, displaced only gradually by the vernacular – in England, by Anglo-Norman French at first, and eventually by English.

variants.name: Latner
meanings: English: occupational name for a worker in or maker of latten or brass, from Middle English latoun ‘brass’ (Old French laton). This name may sometimes have been confused with Latimer .
Altered form of German   Lattner   or   Lettner  .

variants.name: Lattimore
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Latimore
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Lattimer
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .

variants.name: Lattner
meanings: English: variant of   Latimer  .
German: occupational name for a carpenter who prepared or used laths or slats, from Middle High German latte ‘slat, lath’ + -n (plural suffix) + the agent suffix -er.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Thomas le Latimer was born in 1270, in Chipping Warden, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby, was 26 and his mother, Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook, was 20. He married Lora de Hastings about 1292, in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1334, in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64, and was buried in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Thomas le Latimer est né en 1270, à Chipping Warden, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Lora de Hastings environ 1292, à Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1334, à Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Thomas le Latimer 1270 in Chipping Warden, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby 26 und seine Mutter Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook 20. Er hat um 1292 in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England Lora de Hastings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1334 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas le Latimer è nato il nel 1270 nel Chipping Warden, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Lora de Hastings circa 1292, nel Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas le Latimerが1270年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアチップイング ワーデンで生まれた時，父Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danbyは26歳，母Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrookは20歳であった。約1292年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブレイブルークでLora de Hastingsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1334年にイギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブレイブルークで64歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアブレイブルークにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thomas le Latimer(이)가 1270년 영국 노르섬프토실르 치핀그 워든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook(은)는 20세였다. 그는 대략 1292년 영국 노르섬프토실르 브라이브룩에서 Lora de Hastings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1334년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 브라이브룩에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 브라이브룩에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Thomas le Latimer nasceu em 1270, em Chipping Warden, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Lora de Hastings aproximadamente 1292, em Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1334, em Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Thomas le Latimer родился в 1270 г. в местности чиппинг варден, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Lora de Hastings около 1292 г. в местности брэибрук, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер в 1334 г. в местности брэибрук, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности брэибрук, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Thomas le Latimer nació en 1270, en Chipping Warden, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John le Latimer Lord of Danby, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Christian de Ledet Heiress of Baybrook, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Lora de Hastings aproximadamente 1292, en Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en 1334, en Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Braybrooke, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
